أَنْتُمْ– الْمُخَاطَبُ صَحِيْحٌ / سَالِمٌ فِعْلٌ تَامٌ ع ب د عَبَدَ يَعْبُدُ بَابٌ نَصَرَ
Subject – You’ll Sound verb Complete verb Root ن اَلْمَصْدَرُ
فِعْلٌ
Verb of command/request فِعْلُ أَمْرٍ Present and future tense verb فِعْلٌ مُضَارِعٌ Past tense verb فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ
He is worshipping يَعْبُدُ He worshipped عَبَدَ
They two are worshipping يَعْبُدَانِ They two worshipped عَبَدَا
They all are worshipping يَعْبُدُوْنَ They worshipped عَبَدُوْا
She is worshipping تَعْبُدُ She worshipped عَبَدَتْ
They two (feminine) are worshipping تَعْبُدَانِ They two (feminine) worshipped عَبَدَتَا
They all (feminine) are worshipping يَعْبُدْنَ They (feminine) worshipped عَبَدْنَ
Worship (You) اُعْبُدْ You are worshipping تَعْبُدُ You worshipped عَبَدْتَ
Worship (You two) اعْبُدَا You two are worshipping تَعْبُدَانِ You two worshipped عَبَدْتُمَا
Worship (You all) اعْبُدُوْا You all are worshipping تَعْبُدُوْنَ You all worshipped عَبَدْتُمْ
Worship (You feminine) ابُدِي You (feminine) are worshipping تَعْبُدِيْنَ You (feminine) worshipped عَبَدْتِ
Worship (You two feminine) اعْبُدَا You two (feminine) are worshipping تَعْبُدَانِ You two (feminine) worshipped عَبَدْتُمَا
Worship (You all feminine) اعْبُدْنَ You all (feminine) are worshipping تَعْبُدْنَ You all (feminine) worshipped عَبَدْتُنَّ
I am worshipping أَعْبُدُ I worshipped عَبَدْتُ
We are worshipping نَعْبُدُ We worshipped عَبَدْنَا

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section]

content.rendered:
A vocative particle used in calling one who is near and who is far in place or high degree to him who is between near and distant

Root

Occurs approximately 153 times in the holy Quran

143 times as the particle يَآ 

14 times as the particle يَا

 

Surah/Ayat Meaning Aayat
يَا مَعْنًى
2-21 O People, worship your Lord Who has created you and those before you so that you may become God conscious (pious)  يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اعْبُدُوْا رَبَّكُمْ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ وَالَّذِيْنَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقٌوْنَ
He (SWT) said “O Adam! Inform them of their names” and when he had informed them of their names, He (SWT) said قَالَ يَا ءَادَمُ أَنْبِئْهُمْ بِأَسْمَآئِهِمْ فَلَمَّآ أَنْبَأَهُمْ بِأَسْمَآئِهِمْ قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُلْ لَكُمْ
2-33 “Did I not tell you that I know the unseen in the heavens and the earth, and I know what you reveal and what you conceal?” إِنِّي أَعْلَمُ غَيْبَ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالأَرْضِ وَأَعْلَمُ مَا تُبْدُوْنَ وَمَا كُنْتُمْ تَكْتُمُوْنَ
And We said “O Adam! Dwell you and your wife in Paradise and eat both of you freely with pleasure of things therein وَقُلْنَا يَا ءَادَمُ اسْكُنْ أَنْتَ وَزَوْجُكَ الجَنَّةَ وَكُلَا مِنْهَا رَغَدًا حَيْثُ
2-35 as you will but come not near this tree or you both will be of the wrong doers شِئْتُمَا وَلَا تَقْرَبَا هَاذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُوْنَا مِنَ الظَّالِمِيْنَ
109-1 Say, O Disbelievers قُلْ يَآ أَيُّهَا الكَافِرُوْنَ

 

Valuable resources:

يَا occurrence

Dictionary of the holy Quran by Abdul Mannan Omar

Learn Quranic Arabic online dvds

Download study materials, notebooks 1, 2, 3, notes etc

content.rendered:
ش ي أ

Root

 

Surah Meaning Aayat
The root  ش ي أ  occurs in the holy Quran approximately 519 times
فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ 2-20 The lightning almost snatches away their sight, whenever it flashes for them they walk therein however when darkness covers them يَكَادُ البَرْقُ يَخْطَفُ أَبْصَارَهُمْ كُلَّمَا أَضَاءَ لَهُمْ مَشَوْا فِيْهِ وَإِذَا أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ
اِسْمُ الجِنْسِ they stand still. And if Allah willed, He could have taken away their hearing and their sight. Certainly Allah has power over all things قَامُوْا وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللهُ لَذَهَبَ بِسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصَارِهِمْ إِنَّ اللهَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ
2-29 He it is Who created for you all that is on earth, then He established the heaven and proportioned them هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ لَكُمْ مَّا فِي الأَرْضِ جَمِيْعًا ثُمَّ اسْتَوَى إِلَى السَّمَاءِ فَسَوَّاهُنَّ
اِسْمُ الجِنْسِ into seven heavens, He is All Knowing over everything سَبْعَ سَمَوَاتٍ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيْمٌ
فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ 2-35 And We said “O Adam! Dwell you and your wife in Paradise and eat both of you freely with pleasure of things therein وَقُلْنَا يَا ءَادَمُ اسْكُنْ أَنْتَ وَزَوْجُكَ الجَنَّةَ وَكُلَا مِنْهَا رَغَدًا حَيْثُ
as you will but come not near this tree or you both will be of the wrong-doers شِئْتُمَا وَلَا تَقْرَبَا هَاذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُوْنَا مِنَ الظَّالِمِيْنَ

content.rendered:
لَكَ

لَكَ is a phrase (شِبْهُ الجُمْلَةِ) consisting of a particle of pulling down and a genitive noun

Phrase Particle / Noun Noun   Particle
شِبْهُ الجُمْلَةِ جَارٌ وَمَجْرُوْرٌ اسْمٌ مَجْرُوْرٌ حَرْفُ جَرٍّ
Phrase Compound Genitive Noun Particle of pulling down
   
for you لَكَ = كَ + لَ
for you you for/belongs to
Surah/ Below are verses in the holy Quran containing the compound لَكَ
Verse
1
98-1 Have We not opened up for you your breast? أَلَمْ نَشْرَحْ لَكَ صَدْرَكَ
98-4 And have We not raised for you your remembrance? وَرَفَعْنَا لَكَ ذِكْرَكَ

Valuable resources:

لَكَ Corpus.Quran.com

Learn Quranic Arabic online dvds

Download study materials, notebooks 1, 2, 3, notes etc

قَامُوْا

مُعْتَلُّ الْعَيْنِ الغَائِبُ هُمْ   الأَجْوَفُ فِعْلٌ تَامٌّ ق و م قَامَ يَقُوْمُ بَابٌ نَصَرَ
Hollow Subject They Weak Verb Complete Verb Root ن اَلْمَصْدَرُ
فِعْلٌ

I stand أَقُوْمُ I stood قُمْتُ
We stand نَقُوْمُ We stood قُمْنَا

content.rendered:
م و ت

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like م و ت which represents a core meaning or concept

To die, die away (fire), be burn out, become still (wind)

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from م و ت and their meanings

Root

The root م و ت occurs approximately 165 times in the holy Quran

المُمِيْتُ

11 times as the noun المَوْتُ

2 times as the verb مَاتَ

5 times as the verb يَمُوْتُ

 

Surah/ The root م و ت occurs approximately 165 times in the holy Quran م و ت
Verse
or like a rainstorm from the sky wherein is darkness, thunder and lightning أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍ مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ فِيْهِ ظُلُمَاتٌ وَرَعْدٌ وَبَرْقٌ
they thrust their fingers in their ears يَجْعَلُوْنَ أَصَابِعَهُمْ فِي ءَاذَانِهِمْ
2-19 to keep out the stunning thunder-clap for fear of death but Allah SWT ever encompasses the disbelievers مِّنَ الصَّوَاعِقِ حَذَرَ المَوْتِ وَاللهُ مُحِيْطٌ بِالكَافِرِيْنَ
How can you disbelieve in Allah SWT? seeing that you were dead and He gave you life then He will cause you to die, كَيْفَ تَكْفُرُوْنَ بِاللهِ وَكُنْتُمْ أَمْوَاتًا فَأَحْيَاكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيْتُكُمْ
2-28 then again He will bring you to life, then unto Him you will return ثُمَّ يُحْيِيْكُمْ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُوْنَ

75-40 Does He who can do that, not have the power to give life to the dead? أَلَيْسَ ذَلِكَ بِقَادِرٍ عَلَى أَنْ يُحْيِيَ المَوْتَى
77-26 living and dead أَحْيَآءً وَأَمْوَاتًا
80-21 then He causes him to die, then He provides for him a grave ثُمَّ أَمَاتَهُ فَأَقْبَرَهُ
87-13 then, he will neither die in it, nor live ثُمَّ لَا يَمُوتُ فِيهَا وَلَا يَحْيَى

content.rendered:
ح ذ ر

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like ح ذ ر which represents a core meaning or concept

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from ح ذ ر and their meanings

Root

 

Surah/Ayat Meaning Aayat
The root ح ذ ر occurs in the holy Quran approximately 21 times
2-19 or like a rainstorm from the sky wherein is darkness, thunder and lightning. They thrust their fingers in their أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍ مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ فِيْهِ ظُلُمَاتٌ وَرَعْدٌ وَبَرْقٌ يَجْعَلُوْنَ أَصَابِعَهُمْ فِي ءَاذَانِهِمْ
مَاضٍ فِعْلٌ ears to keep out the lightning strikes for fear of death but Allah ever encompasses the disbelievers مِّنَ الصَّوَاعِقِ حَذَرَ المَوْتِ وَاللهُ مُحِيْطٌ بِالكَافِرِيْنَ

مُضَارِعٌ فِعْلٌ Say, “Mock, indeed, Allah (SWT) will bring forth what you fear” قُلِ اسْتَهْزِءُوا إِنَّ اللهَ مُخْرِجٌ مَّا تَحْذَرُوْنَ

فِعْلٌ

I make أَجْعَلُ I made جَعَلْتُ
We make نَجْعَلُ We made جَعَلْنَا

content.rendered:
ب ر ق

Root

 

Surah Meaning Aayat
Ayat مَعْنًى
الْمَصْدَرُ 2-19 or like a rainstorm from the sky wherein is darkness, thunder and lightning. They thrust their fingers in their أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍ مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ فِيْهِ ظُلُمَاتٌ وَرَعْدٌ وَبَرْقٌ يَجْعَلُوْنَ أَصَابِعَهُمْ فِي ءَاذَانِهِمْ
ears to keep out the stunning thunder-clap for fear of death but Allah ever encompasses the disbelievers مِّنَ الصَّوَاعِقِ حَذَرَ المَوْتِ وَاللهُ مُحِيْطٌ بِالكَافِرِيْنَ
الْمَصْدَرُ 2-20 The lightning almost snatches away their sight, whenever it flashes for them they walk therein however when darkness covers them يَكَادُ البَرْقُ يَخْطَفُ أَبْصَارَهُمْ كُلَّمَا أَضَاءَ لَهُمْ مَشَوْا فِيْهِ وَإِذَا أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ
they stand still. And if Allah willed, He could have taken away their hearing and their sight. Certainly Allah has power over all things قَامُوْا وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللهُ لَذَهَبَ بِسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصَارِهِمْ إِنَّ اللهَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ

content.rendered:
كَ root

كَ is an attached pronoun of the second person meaning you

كَ is also a particle (preposition) prefixed to nouns and other particles to mean as or like

Root

Occurs approximately 1+ times in the holy Quran

2+ times as the second person pronoun كَ

1+ times as a preposition prefixed to nouns and other particles كَ

 

Surah Meaning Aayat
Verse حَرْفٌ مَعْنًى
حَرْفُ التَّشْبِيَّةِ 2-19 or like a rainstorm from the sky wherein is darkness, thunder and lightning. They thrust their fingers in their أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍ مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ فِيْهِ ظُلُمَاتٌ وَرَعَدٌ وَبَرْقٌ يَجْعَلُوْنَ أَصَابِعَهُمْ فِي ءَاذَانِهِمْ
ears to keep out the stunning thunderclap for fear of death but Allah ever encompasses the disbelievers مِّنَ الصَّوَاعِقَ حَذَرَ المَوْتِ وَاللهُ مُحِيْطٌ بِالكَافِرِيْنَ
ضَمِيْرٌ 108-1 Surely, We have given you The Fountain of Abundance إِنَّآ أَعْطَيْنَاكَ الْكَوْثَرَ
ضَمِيْرٌ 110-3 Then glorify the praises of your Nurturer and seek His forgiveness, truly He is oft returning فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَاسْتَغْفِرْهُ إِنَّهُ كَانَ تَوَّابًا

يَرْجِعُوْنَ

هُمْ الْغَائِبُ صَحِيْحٌ / سَالِمٌ فِعْلٌ تَامٌ ر ج ع رَجَعَ يَرْجِعُ بَابٌ ضَرَبَ
Subject – They Sound verb Complete verb Root ض اَلْمَصْدَرُ
فِعْلٌ
Verb of command/request فِعْلُ أَمْرٍ Present and future tense verb فِعْلٌ مُضَارِعٌ Past tense verb فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ
He returns يَرْجِعُ He returned رَجَعَ
They two return يَرْجِعَانِ They two returned رَجَعَا
They all return يَرْجِعُوْنَ They returned رَجَعُوْا
She returns تَرْجِعُ She returned رَجَعَتْ

Return (You all feminine) اِرْجِعْنَ You all (feminine) return تَرْجِعْنَ You all (feminine) returned رَجَعْتُنَّ
I return أَرْجِعُ I returned رَجَعْتُ
We return نَرْجِعُ We returned رَجَعْنَا

content.rendered:
ظ ل م

Root

 

Surah/Ayat Meaning Aayat
The root  ظ ل م  occurs in the holy Quran approximately 315 times
الْجَمْعُ الْمُؤَنَّثُ السَّالِمُ اسْمٌ 2-17 Their likeness is as the likeness of one who kindled a fire, then, when it lighted all around him, مَثَلُهُمْ كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي اسْتَوْقَدَ نَارًا فَلَمَّا أَضَائَتْ مَا حَوْلَهُ ذَهَبَ اللهُ بِنُوْرِهِمْ
Allah took away their light and left them in darkness so they could not see وَتَرَكَهُمْ فِي ظُلُمَاتٍ لَّا يُبْصِرُوْنَ
الْجَمْعُ الْمُؤَنَّثُ السَّالِمُ اسْمٌ 2-19 or like a rainstorm from the sky wherein is darkness, thunder and lightning. They thrust their fingers in  أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍ مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ فِيْهِ ظُلُمَاتٌ وَرَعَدٌ وَبَرْقٌ يَجْعَلُوْنَ أَصَابِعَهُمْ فِي ءَاذَانِهِمْ
their ears to keep out the thunder-clap for fear of death but Allah ever encompasses the disbelievers مِّنَ الصَّوَاعِقِ حَذَرَ المَوْتِ وَاللهُ مُحِيْطٌ بِالكَافِرِيْنَ
فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ F4 2-20 The lightning almost snatches away their sight, whenever it flashes for them they walk therein however when darkness covers them يَكَادُ البَرْقُ يَخْطَفُ أَبْصَارَهُمْ كُلَّمَا أَضَاءَ لَهُمْ مَشَوْا فِيْهِ وَإِذَا أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ
they stand still. And if Allah willed, He could have taken away their hearing and their sight. Certainly Allah has power over all things قَامُوْا وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللهُ لَذَهَبَ بِسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصَارِهِمْ إِنَّ اللهَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ
2-35 And We said “O Adam! Dwell you and your wife in Paradise and eat both of you freely with pleasure of things therein وَقُلْنَا يَا ءَادَمُ اسْكُنْ أَنْتَ وَزَوْجُكَ الجَنَّةَ وَكُلَا مِنْهَا رَغَدًا حَيْثُ
الْجَمْعُ المُذَكَّرُ السَّالِمُ اسْمُ الفَاعِلِ as you will but come not near this tree or you both will be of the wrong-doers شِئْتُمَا وَلَا تَقْرَبَا هَاذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُوْنَا مِنَ الظَّالِمِيْنَ
2-51 And when we appointed for Moses (RA) forty nights thereafter you took the calf وَإِذْ وَاعَدْنَا مُوْسَى أَرْبَعِيْنَ لَيْلَةً ثُمَّ اتَّخَذْتُمُ العِجْلَ
الْجَمْعُ المُذَكَّرُ السَّالِمُ اسْمُ الفَاعِلِ for worship so you were wrongdoers مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَأَنْتُمْ ظَالِمُوْنَ

تَرَكَ

هُوَ– الْغَائِبُ صَحِيْحٌ / سَالِمٌ فِعْلٌ تَامٌ ت ر ك خَلَقَ يَخْلُقُ بَابٌ نَصَرَ
Subject – He Sound verb Complete verb Root ن اَلْمَصْدَرُ
فِعْلٌ
Verb of command/request فِعْلُ أَمْرٍ Present and future tense verb فِعْلٌ مُضَارِعٌ Past tense verb فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ
He leaves يَتْرُكُ He left تَرَكَ
They two leave يَتْرُكَانِ They two left تَرَكَا

Leave (You all feminine) اُتْرُكْنَ You all (feminine) leave تَتْرُكْنَ You all (feminine) left تَرَكْتُنَّ
I leave أَتْرُكُ I left تَرَكْتُ
We leave نَتْرُكُ We left تَرَكْنَا

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like ت ر ك which represents a core meaning or concept

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from ت ر ك and their meanings

content.rendered:
ذ ه ب

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like ذ ه ب which represents a core meaning or concept

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from ذ ه ب and their meanings

Root

 

Pattern Surah/ Meaning / Occurrence Aayat
Verse The root ذ ه ب occurs in the holy Quran approximately 56 times
فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ 2-17 Their likeness is as the likeness of one who kindled a fire, then, when it lighted all around him, Allah took away مَثَلُهُمْ كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي اسْتَوْقَدَ نَارًا فَلَمَّا أَضَائَتْ مَا حَوْلَهُ ذَهَبَ اللهُ بِنُوْرِهِمْ
their light and left them in darkness so they could not see وَتَرَكَهُمْ فِي ظُلُمَاتٍ لَّا يُبْصِرُوْنَ
2-20 The lightning almost snatches away their sight, whenever it flashes for them, they walk therein however when darkness covers them يَكَادُ البَرْقُ يَخْطَفُ أَبْصَارَهُمْ كُلَّمَا أَضَاءَ لَهُمْ مَشَوْا فِيْهِ وَإِذَا أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ
فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ they stand still. And if Allah willed, He could have certainly taken away their hearing and their sight. Verily, Allah has power over all things قَامُوْا وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللهُ لَذَهَبَ بِسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصَارِهِمْ إِنَّ اللهَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ

content.rendered:
ض و أ

Root

 

Surah/Ayat Meaning Aayat
مَعْنًى
فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ فِعْلٌ 2-17 Their likeness is as the likeness of one who kindled a fire, then, when it lighted all around him, Allah took away مَثَلُهُمْ كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي اسْتَوْقَدَ نَارًا فَلَمَّا أَضَاءَتْ مَا حَوْلَهُ ذَهَبَ اللهُ بِنُوْرِهِمْ
their light and left them in darkness so they could not see وَتَرَكَهُمْ فِي ظُلُمَاتٍ لَّا يُبْصِرُوْنَ
فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ فِعْلٌ 2-20 The lightning almost snatches away their sight, whenever it flashes for them they walk therein however when darkness covers them يَكَادُ البَرْقُ يَخْطَفُ أَبْصَارَهُمْ كُلَّمَا أَضَاءَ لَهُمْ مَشَوْا فِيْهِ وَإِذَا أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ
they stand still. And if Allah willed, He could have taken away their hearing and their sight. Certainly Allah has power over all things قَامُوْا وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللهُ لَذَهَبَ بِسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصَارِهِمْ إِنَّ اللهَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ

لَمَّا ظَرْفٌ root

Root

 

Surah/Ayat Meaning Aayat
لَمَّا اسْمٌ ظَرْفُ الْزَّمَانِ
2-17 Their likeness is as the likeness of one who kindled a fire, then, when it lighted all around him, Allah took away مَثَلُهُمْ كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي اسْتَوْقَدَ نَارًا فَلَمَّا أَضَاءَتْ مَا حَوْلَهُ ذَهَبَ اللهُ بِنُوْرِهِمْ
their light and left them in darkness so they could not see وَتَرَكَهُمْ فِي ظُلُمَاتٍ لَّا يُبْصِرُوْنَ
2-33 He (SWT) said “O Adam! Inform them of their names” and when he had informed them of their names, He (SWT) said قَالَ يَا ءَادَمُ أَنْبِئْهُمْ بِأَسْمَآئِهِمْ فَلَمَّآ أَنْبَأَهُمْ بِأَسْمَآئِهِمْ قَالَ أَلَمْ أَقُلْ لَكُمْ
“Did I not tell you that I know the unseen in the heavens and the earth and I know what you reveal and what you conceal?”  إِنِّي أَعْلَمُ غَيْبَ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالأَرْضِ وَأَعْلَمُ مَا تُبْدُوْنَ وَمَا كُنْتُمْ تَكْتُمُوْنَ

content.rendered:
الهُدَى مَجْرُوْرٌ

A word in the Arabic language will either be 1) a verb or 2) a noun or 3) a particle – الهُدَى مَجْرُوْرٌ is a noun

A noun may either be 1) Definite or indefinite 2) Masculine or feminine 3) Singular, dual or plural 4) Nominative, accusative or genitive

الهُدَى is the natural state of this definite noun and it becomes الهُدَى due to: 1) a preposition that precedes it or 2) a possessed noun that precedes it or 3) it’s a noun of dependent declension a) نَعْتٌ / صِفَطٌ adjectives b) التَّوْكِيْدُ emphatic c) المَعْطُوْفٌ conjunction d) البَدَلُ synonym

Due to this noun being weak, ( its third radical is a weak letter) its third radical does not show its grammatical changes so its changes must be estimated (seen in the mind)

Guidance. A direction that indicates the right way

To guide, show with kindness the right path (90:10), lead to the right path (29:69) and to make one follow the right path till one reaches the goal (7:43)

مَعْرِفَةٌ مُفْرَدٌ مُذَكَّرٌ مَجْرُوْرٌ ه د ي بَاب ضَرَبَ هَدَى يَهْدِي فُعَلٌ اسْمٌ الْمَصْدَرُ
Definite Singular Masculine Genitive Root Guidance ض Pattern Noun Verbal noun
Surah/ Below are the 17 verses in the holy Quran containing the definite,  genitive noun الهُدَى
Verse
Those are they who purchased error at the price of guidance أُوْلَآئِكَ الَّذِيْنَ اشْتَرَوُا الضَّلَالَةَ بِالهُدَى
2-16 so, not profitable was their commerce as they were not guided فَمَا رَبِحَتْ تِجَارَةُهُمْ وَمَا كَانُوْا مُهْتَدِيْنَ

96-11 Do you consider that he is on the guidance? أَرَءَيْتَ إِنْ كَانَ عَلَى الهُدَى

content.rendered:
اشْتَرَوْا

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Form 8 is the reflexive or middle voice (مُطَاوِعٌ) of form 1. The reflex object is either a) the direct object or accusative or b) the indirect object or dative implying for oneself or for one’s own advantage. Reflexive verbs describe actions that a subject or subjects perform on itself or themselves using reflexive pronouns like myself, ourselves, yourself, him or herself, itself etc

2) It is common in this form to express reciprocity (describing actions that two or more people perform on each other)

3) Occasionally the original reflexive meaning passes into the passive, especially in verbs which do not have form 7

4) In a few verbs form 8 and form 1 agree so closely that they may be translated by the same word

هُمْ – الْغَائِبُ صَحِيْحٌ / سَالِمٌ فِعْلٌ تَامٌ ع م ه عَمَهَ يَعْمَهُ بَابٌ فَتَحَ

I wander blindly أَعْمَهُ I wandered blindly عَمَهْتُ
We wander blindly نَعْمَهُ We wandered blindly عَمَهْنَا

content.rendered:
يَمُدُّ

هُوَ– الْغَائِبُ الْمُضَعَّفُ فِعْلٌ تَامٌ م د د  مَدَّ يَمُدُّ بَابٌ نَصَرَ
Subject – He Muda’af
 Complete verb Root ن اَلْمَصْدَرُ
فِعْلٌ
Verb of command/request فِعْلُ أَمْرٍ Present and future tense verb فِعْلٌ مُضَارِعٌ Past tense verb فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ
He prolongs يَمُدُّ He prolonged مَدَّ
They two prolong يَمُدَّانِ They two prolonged مَدَّا
They all prolong يَمُدُّوْنَ They prolonged مَدُّوْا
She prolongs تَمُدُّ She prolonged مَدَّتْ
They two (feminine) prolong تَمُدَّانِ They two (feminine) prolonged مَدَّتَا
They all (feminine) prolong يَمْدُدْنَ They (feminine) prolonged مَدَدْنَ
Prolong (You) مُدَّ You prolong تَمُدُّ You prolonged مَدَدْتَ
Prolong (You two) مُدَّا You two prolong تَمُدَّانِ You two prolonged مَدّدْتُمَا
Prolong (You all) مُدُّوْا You all prolong تَمُدُّوْنَ You all prolonged مَدَدْتُمْ
Prolong (You feminine) مُدِّي You (feminine) prolong تَمُدِّيْنَ You (feminine) prolonged مَدَدْتِ
Prolong (You two feminine) مُدَّا You two (feminine) prolong تَمُدَّانِ You two (feminine) prolonged مَدَدْتُمَا
Prolong (You all feminine) أُمْدُدْنَ You all (feminine) prolong تَمْدُدْنَ You all (feminine) prolonged مَدَدْتُنَّ
I prolong أَمُدُّ I prolonged مَدَدْتُ
We prolong نَمُدُّ We urged مَدَدْنَا

content.rendered:
ه ز أ

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like ه ز أ which represents a core meaning or concept

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from ه ز أ and their meanings

Root

 

Pattern Surah/ Meaning Aayat
Verse The root ه ز أ occurs in the holy Quran approximately 34 times
الْجَمْعُ الْمُذَكَّرُ السَّالِمُ اسْمٌ 2-14 And when they meet the believers, they say “We believe” but when they are alone with their disbelievers  وَإِذَا لَقُوْا الَّذِيْنَ ءَامَنُوْا قَالُوْا ءَامَنَّا وَإِذَا خَلَوْا إِلَى شَيَاطِيْنِهِمْ
اسْمٌ فَاعِلٌ F10 they say “Truly we are with you, truly we were but mockers” قَالُوْا إِنَّا مَعَكُمْ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُسْتَهْزِءُوْنَ
فِعْلٌ مُضَارِعٌ فِعْلٌ 2-15 Allah mocks at them and increases them in their wrongdoing to wander blindly اللهُ يَسْتَهْزِئُ بِهِمْ وَ يَمُدُّهُمْ فِيْ طُغْيَانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُوْنَ

مُسْتَهْزِءُوْنَ

النَّكِرَةُ جَمْعٌ مُذَكَّرٌ مَرْفُوْعٌ ه ز أ بَاب اِسْتِفْعَالٌ اِسْتَهْزَءَ يَسْتَهْزِئُ مُسْتَفْعِلُوْنَ اسْمٌ الْجَمْعُ الْمُذَكَّرُ السَّالِمُ
Indefinite Plural Masculine Nominative Root Mockers Form 10 Pattern Noun Sound masculine plural
اِسْمُ الْفَاعِلُ
Active participle

Meet pleased (You two feminine) اِلْقَيَا You two (feminine) meet تَلْقَيَانِ You two (feminine) met لَقِيْتُمَا
Meet pleased (You all feminine) اِلْقَيْنَ You all (feminine) meet تَلْقَيْنَ You all (feminine) met لَقِيْتُنَّ
I meet أَلْقَى I met لَقِيْتُ
We meet نَلْقَى We met لَقِيْنَا

content.rendered:
ل ق ي

Root

 

Surah   Meaning Aayat
The root  ل ق ي  occurs in the holy Quran approximately 146 times
فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ فِعْلٌ 2-14 And when they meet the believers, they say “We believe” but when they are alone with their disbelievers  وَإِذَا لَقُوْا الَّذِيْنَ ءَامَنُوْا قَالُوْا ءَامَنَّا وَإِذَا خَلَوْا إِلَى شَيَاطِيْنِهِمْ
they say “Truly we are with you, truly we were but mocking” قَالُوْا إِنَّا مَعَكُمْ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُسْتَهْزِءُوْنَ
فِعْلٌ مَاضٍ F5 2-37 Then Adam received from his Lord Words and his Lord pardoned him, Truly He is the Acceptor of repentance, Most Merciful فَتَلَقَّى ءَادَمُ مِنْ رَّبِّهِ كَلِمَاتٍ فَتَابَ عَلَيْهِ إِنَّهُ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيْمُ
اسْمُ الْفَاعِلِ F3 2-46 Those who are convinced in their meeting with their Lord and that الَّذِيْنَ يَظُنُّوْنَ أَنَّهُمْ مُلَاقُوْا رَبِّهِمْ وَأَنَّهُمْ
unto Him they will be returning  إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُوْنَ
